selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.

response_raw: {"mode":"human","topic":"How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.","opinion":"Managing fake content on social media is more than just spotting lies; it’s about being vigilant, curious, and proactive. response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: Managing fake content on social media is more than just spotting lies; it’s about being vigilant, curious, and proactive. As someone who spends a few times weekly on these platforms, I know the importance of remaining skeptical of every post that catches my eye. My curiosity often leads me to ask questions—how was this image taken? When did this video go viral? Who shared it first? These simple steps can help avoid spreading misinformation.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: As a retail worker, I see customers daily who are influenced by social media without critically evaluating sources. In my experience, people often believe what they read without checking credibility. To combat this, I have adopted the habit of cross-referencing information with trusted news outlets or official sources before sharing it on my own feed. Additionally, I make sure to report any suspicious content to the platform—this not only helps protect others but also keeps me informed about trends in disinformation. My low openness and conscientiousness scores suggest a preference for practical solutions over abstract theories, so I focus on actionable steps such as fact-checking before reacting. I also try to engage with communities that prioritize factual accuracy and critical thinking, which reinforces my own skepticism.
response_parsed.tone: Cautious yet pragmatic; grounded in experience but open to learning more.
